constructors.name: ViewportInfo(RhinoViewport)
constructors.summary: Copies all of the ViewportInfo data from an existing RhinoViewport.

constructors.name: ViewportInfo(ViewportInfo)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance by copying values from another instance.

methods.name: CalculateCameraRotationAngle
methods.signature: public static double CalculateCameraRotationAngle(Vector3d direction, Vector3d up)
methods.summary: Calculates the camera rotation angle.

returns.doc: The camera rotation angle in radians.

methods.name: CalculateCameraUpDirection
methods.signature: public static Vector3d CalculateCameraUpDirection(Point3d location, Vector3d direction, double angle)
methods.summary: Calculates the camera up direction.

params.name: angle
params.doc: The camera rotation angle in radians.

returns.doc: The camera up direction.

methods.name: ChangeToParallelProjection
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToParallelProjection(bool symmetricFrustum)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports from parallel to perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location so the resulting views are similar. The camera direction and target point are not be changed. If the current projection is parallel and symmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

params.name: symmetricFrustum
params.doc: true if you want the resulting frustum to be symmetric.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: ChangeToPerspectiveProjection
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToPerspectiveProjection(double targetDistance, bool symmetricFrustum, double lensLength)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports from parallel to perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location so the resulting views are similar. The camera direction and target point are not changed. If the current projection is perspective and symmetricFrustum, IsFrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric, and IsFrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If RhinoMath.UnsetValue this parameter is ignored. Otherwise it must be > 0 and indicates which plane in the current view frustum should be preserved.

params.name: symmetricFrustum
params.doc: true if you want the resulting frustum to be symmetric.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: ChangeToSymmetricFrustum
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToSymmetricFrustum(bool isLeftRightSymmetric, bool isTopBottomSymmetric, double targetDistance)
methods.summary: If needed, adjusts the current frustum so it has the specified symmetries and adjust the camera location so the target plane remains visible.

params.name: isLeftRightSymmetric
params.doc: If true, the frustum will be adjusted so left = -right.

params.name: isTopBottomSymmetric
params.doc: If true, the frustum will be adjusted so top = -bottom.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If projection is not perspective or target_distance is RhinoMath.UnsetValue, then this parameter is ignored. If the projection is perspective and targetDistance is not RhinoMath.UnsetValue, then it must be > 0.0 and it is used to determine which plane in the old frustum will appear unchanged in the new frustum.

returns.doc: Returns true if the viewport has now a frustum with the specified symmetries.

methods.name: ChangeToTwoPointPerspectiveProjection
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToTwoPointPerspectiveProjection(double targetDistance, Vector3d up, double lensLength)
methods.summary: Changes projections of valid viewports to a two point perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location and direction so the resulting views are similar. If the current projection is perspective and IsFrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric is true and IsFrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric is false, then no changes are made and true is returned.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If RhinoMath.UnsetValue this parameter is ignored. Otherwise it must be > 0 and indicates which plane in the current view frustum should be preserved.

params.name: up
params.doc: The locked up direction. Pass Vector3d.Zero if you want to use the world axis direction that is closest to the current up direction. Pass CameraY() if you want to preserve the current up direction.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.signature: public void Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: DollyCamera
methods.signature: public bool DollyCamera(Vector3d dollyVector)
methods.summary: DollyCamera() does not update the frustum's clipping planes. To update the frustum's clipping planes call DollyFrustum(d) with d = dollyVector o cameraFrameZ. To convert screen locations into a dolly vector, use GetDollyCameraVector(). Does not update frustum. To update frustum use DollyFrustum(d) with d = dollyVector o cameraFrameZ.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: DollyExtents(BoundingBox, Double)
methods.signature: public bool DollyExtents(BoundingBox cameraCoordinateBoundingBox, double border)
methods.summary: Dolly the camera location and so that the view frustum contains all of the document objects that can be seen in view. If the projection is perspective, the camera angle is not changed.

params.name: border
params.doc: If border > 1.0, then the frustum in enlarged by this factor to provide a border around the view. 1.1 works well for parallel projections; 0.0 is suggested for perspective projections.

returns.doc: True if successful.

methods.name: DollyExtents(IEnumerable<GeometryBase>, Double)
methods.signature: public bool DollyExtents(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, double border)
methods.summary: Dolly the camera location and so that the view frustum contains all of the document objects that can be seen in view. If the projection is perspective, the camera angle is not changed.

params.name: border
params.doc: If border > 1.0, then the frustum in enlarged by this factor to provide a border around the view. 1.1 works well for parallel projections; 0.0 is suggested for perspective projections.

returns.doc: True if successful.

methods.name: DollyFrustum
methods.signature: public bool DollyFrustum(double dollyDistance)
methods.summary: Moves the frustum clipping planes.

params.name: dollyDistance
params.doc: Distance to move in camera direction.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.signature: public void EnsurePrivateCopy()
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: Extents(Double, BoundingBox)
methods.signature: public bool Extents(double halfViewAngleRadians, BoundingBox bbox)
methods.summary: Extends this viewport view to include a bounding box. Use Extents() as a quick way to set a viewport to so that bounding volume is inside of a viewports frustum. The view angle is used to determine the position of the camera.

params.name: halfViewAngleRadians
params.doc: 1/2 smallest subtended view angle in radians.

params.name: bbox
params.doc: A bounding box in 3d world coordinates.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Extents(Double, Sphere)
methods.signature: public bool Extents(double halfViewAngleRadians, Sphere sphere)
methods.summary: Extends this viewport view to include a sphere. Use Extents() as a quick way to set a viewport to so that bounding volume is inside of a viewports frustum. The view angle is used to determine the position of the camera.

params.name: halfViewAngleRadians
params.doc: 1/2 smallest subtended view angle in radians.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: FrustumCenterPoint
methods.signature: public Point3d FrustumCenterPoint(double targetDistance)
methods.summary: Return a point on the central axis of the view frustum. This point is a good choice for a general purpose target point.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If targetDistance > 0.0, then the distance from the returned point to the camera plane will be targetDistance. Note that if the frustum is not symmetric, the distance from the returned point to the camera location will be larger than targetDistance. If targetDistance == ON_UNSET_VALUE and the frustum is valid with near > 0.0, then 0.5*(near + far) will be used as the targetDistance.

returns.doc: A point on the frustum's central axis. If the viewport or input is not valid, then ON_3dPoint::UnsetPoint is returned.

methods.name: GetBoundingBoxDepth
methods.signature: public bool GetBoundingBoxDepth(BoundingBox bbox, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets near and far clipping distances of a bounding box. This function ignores the current value of the viewport's near and far settings. If the viewport is a perspective projection, the it intersects the semi infinite frustum volume with the bounding box and returns the near and far distances of the intersection. If the viewport is a parallel projection, it intersects the infinite view region with the bounding box and returns the near and far distances of the projection.

params.name: nearDistance
params.doc: Near distance of the box. This value can be zero or negative when the camera location is inside box.

params.name: farDistance
params.doc: Far distance of the box. This value can be equal to near_dist, zero or negative when the camera location is in front of the bounding box.

returns.doc: true if the bounding box intersects the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the bounding box does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetCameraAngles
methods.signature: public bool GetCameraAngles(out double halfDiagonalAngleRadians, out double halfVerticalAngleRadians, out double halfHorizontalAngleRadians)
methods.summary: Gets the field of view angles.

params.name: halfDiagonalAngleRadians
params.doc: 1/2 of diagonal subtended angle. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

params.name: halfVerticalAngleRadians
params.doc: 1/2 of vertical subtended angle. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

params.name: halfHorizontalAngleRadians
params.doc: 1/2 of horizontal subtended angle. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GetCameraFrame
methods.signature: public bool GetCameraFrame(out Point3d location, out Vector3d cameraX, out Vector3d cameraY, out Vector3d cameraZ)
methods.summary: Gets location and vectors of this camera.

params.name: location
params.doc: An out parameter that will be filled with a point during the call.

params.name: cameraX
params.doc: An out parameter that will be filled with the X vector during the call.

params.name: cameraY
params.doc: An out parameter that will be filled with the Y vector during the call.

params.name: cameraZ
params.doc: An out parameter that will be filled with the Z vector during the call.

returns.doc: true if current camera orientation is valid; otherwise false.

methods.name: GetDollyCameraVector(Point, Point, Double)
methods.signature: public Vector3d GetDollyCameraVector(Point screen0, Point screen1, double projectionPlaneDistance)
methods.summary: Gets a world coordinate dolly vector that can be passed to DollyCamera().

params.name: projectionPlaneDistance
params.doc: Distance of projection plane from camera. When in doubt, use 0.5*(frus_near+frus_far).

returns.doc: The world coordinate dolly vector.

methods.name: GetDollyCameraVector(Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32, Double)
methods.signature: public Vector3d GetDollyCameraVector(int screenX0, int screenY0, int screenX1, int screenY1, double projectionPlaneDistance)
methods.summary: Gets a world coordinate dolly vector that can be passed to DollyCamera().

params.name: projectionPlaneDistance
params.doc: Distance of projection plane from camera. When in doubt, use 0.5*(frus_near+frus_far).

returns.doc: The world coordinate dolly vector.

methods.name: GetFarPlaneCorners
methods.signature: public Point3d[] GetFarPlaneCorners()
methods.summary: Gets the corners of far clipping plane rectangle. 4 points are returned in the order of bottom left, bottom right, top left, top right.

returns.doc: Four corner points on success. Empty array if viewport is not valid.

methods.name: GetFrustum
methods.signature: public bool GetFrustum(out double left, out double right, out double bottom, out double top, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets the view frustum.

params.name: left
params.doc: A left value that will be filled during the call.

params.name: right
params.doc: A right value that will be filled during the call.

params.name: bottom
params.doc: A bottom value that will be filled during the call.

params.name: top
params.doc: A top value that will be filled during the call.

params.name: nearDistance
params.doc: A near distance value that will be filled during the call.

params.name: farDistance
params.doc: A far distance value that will be filled during the call.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GetFrustumLine(Point)
methods.signature: public Line GetFrustumLine(Point screenPoint)
methods.summary: Gets the world coordinate line in the view frustum that projects to a point on the screen.

returns.doc: 3d world coordinate line segment starting on the near clipping plane and ending on the far clipping plane.

methods.name: GetFrustumLine(PointF)
methods.signature: public Line GetFrustumLine(PointF screenPoint)
methods.summary: Gets the world coordinate line in the view frustum that projects to a point on the screen.

returns.doc: 3d world coordinate line segment starting on the near clipping plane and ending on the far clipping plane.

methods.name: GetFrustumLine(Double, Double)
methods.signature: public Line GetFrustumLine(double screenX, double screenY)
methods.summary: Gets the world coordinate line in the view frustum that projects to a point on the screen.

returns.doc: 3d world coordinate line segment starting on the near clipping plane and ending on the far clipping plane.

methods.name: GetNearPlaneCorners
methods.signature: public Point3d[] GetNearPlaneCorners()
methods.summary: Gets the corners of near clipping plane rectangle. 4 points are returned in the order of bottom left, bottom right, top left, top right.

returns.doc: Four corner points on success. Empty array if viewport is not valid.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public virtual void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

methods.name: GetPointDepth
methods.signature: public bool GetPointDepth(Point3d point, out double distance)
methods.summary: Gets the clipping distance of a point. This function ignores the current value of the viewport's near and far settings. If the viewport is a perspective projection, then it intersects the semi infinite frustum volume with the bounding box and returns the near and far distances of the intersection. If the viewport is a parallel projection, it intersects the infinite view region with the bounding box and returns the near and far distances of the projection.

returns.doc: true if the bounding box intersects the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the bounding box does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetScreenPort()
methods.signature: public Rectangle GetScreenPort()
methods.summary: Gets the location of viewport in pixels. See documentation for SetScreenPort.

returns.doc: The rectangle, or Empty rectangle on error.

methods.name: GetScreenPort(Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public Rectangle GetScreenPort(out int near, out int far)
methods.summary: Gets the location of viewport in pixels. See value meanings in SetScreenPort.

params.name: near
params.doc: The near value. This out parameter is assigned during the call.

params.name: far
params.doc: The far value. This out parameter is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: The rectangle, or Empty rectangle on error.

methods.name: GetScreenPortLocation
methods.signature: public void GetScreenPortLocation(out int left, out int top, out int right, out int bottom)
methods.summary: Get the location of viewport in pixels (non System.Drawing version of GetScreenPort)

methods.name: GetSphereDepth
methods.signature: public bool GetSphereDepth(Sphere sphere, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets near and far clipping distances of a bounding sphere.

params.name: nearDistance
params.doc: Near distance of the sphere (can be < 0)

params.name: farDistance
params.doc: Far distance of the sphere (can be equal to near_dist)

returns.doc: true if the sphere intersects the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the sphere does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetWorldToScreenScale
methods.signature: public double GetWorldToScreenScale(Point3d pointInFrustum)
methods.summary: Gets the scale factor from point in frustum to screen scale.

returns.doc: number of pixels per world unit at the 3d point.

methods.name: GetXform
methods.signature: public Transform GetXform(CoordinateSystem sourceSystem, CoordinateSystem destinationSystem)
methods.summary: Computes a transform from a coordinate system to another.

params.name: sourceSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to map from.

params.name: destinationSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to map into.

returns.doc: The 4x4 transformation matrix (acts on the left).

methods.name: IsValidWithLog
methods.signature: public bool IsValidWithLog(out string log)
methods.summary: Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

params.name: log
params.doc: A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

methods.name: RotateCamera
methods.signature: public bool RotateCamera(double rotationAngleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)
methods.summary: Rotates the view camera.

params.name: rotationAngleRadians
params.doc: The amount to rotate expressed in radians.

returns.doc: True if rotation is successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: SetCameraDirection
methods.signature: public bool SetCameraDirection(Vector3d direction)
methods.summary: Sets the direction that the camera faces.

returns.doc: true if the direction was set; otherwise false.

methods.name: SetCameraLocation
methods.signature: public bool SetCameraLocation(Point3d location)
methods.summary: Sets the camera location (position) point.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetCameraUp
methods.signature: public bool SetCameraUp(Vector3d up)
methods.summary: Sets the camera up vector.

returns.doc: true if the direction was set; otherwise false.

methods.name: SetFrustum
methods.signature: public bool SetFrustum(double left, double right, double bottom, double top, double nearDistance, double farDistance)
methods.summary: Sets the view frustum. If FrustumSymmetryIsLocked() is true and left != -right or bottom != -top, then they will be adjusted so the resulting frustum is symmetric.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetFrustumNearFar(BoundingBox)
methods.signature: public bool SetFrustumNearFar(BoundingBox boundingBox)
methods.summary: Sets the frustum near and far using a bounding box.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetFrustumNearFar(Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetFrustumNearFar(double nearDistance, double farDistance)
methods.summary: Sets the frustum near and far distances using two values.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetFrustumNearFar(Point3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetFrustumNearFar(Point3d center, double radius)
methods.summary: Sets the frustum near and far using a center point and radius.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetFrustumNearFar(Double, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetFrustumNearFar(double nearDistance, double farDistance, double minNearDistance, double minNearOverFar, double targetDistance)
methods.summary: Sets near and far clipping distance subject to constraints.

params.name: minNearDistance
params.doc: If min_near_dist <= 0.0, it is ignored. If min_near_dist > 0 and near_dist < min_near_dist, then the frustum's near_dist will be increased to min_near_dist.

params.name: minNearOverFar
params.doc: If min_near_over_far <= 0.0, it is ignored. If near_dist < far_dist*min_near_over_far, then near_dist is increased and/or far_dist is decreased so that near_dist = far_dist*min_near_over_far. If near_dist < target_dist < far_dist, then near_dist near_dist is increased and far_dist is decreased so that projection precision will be good at target_dist. Otherwise, near_dist is simply set to far_dist*min_near_over_far.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If target_dist <= 0.0, it is ignored. If target_dist > 0, it is used as described in the description of the min_near_over_far parameter.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: SetScreenPort(Rectangle)
methods.signature: public bool SetScreenPort(Rectangle windowRectangle)
methods.summary: Gets the location of viewport in pixels. See value meanings in SetScreenPort.

returns.doc: true if input is valid.

methods.name: SetScreenPort(Rectangle, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public bool SetScreenPort(Rectangle windowRectangle, int near, int far)
methods.summary: Gets the location of viewport in pixels. See value meanings in SetScreenPort.

returns.doc: true if input is valid.

methods.name: SetScreenPort(Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public bool SetScreenPort(int left, int right, int bottom, int top, int near, int far)
methods.summary: Location of viewport in pixels. These are provided so you can set the port you are using and get the appropriate transformations to and from screen space. // For a Windows window / int width = width of window client area in pixels; / int height = height of window client area in pixels; / port_left = 0; / port_right = width; / port_top = 0; / port_bottom = height; / port_near = 0; / port_far = 1; / SetScreenPort( port_left, port_right, / port_bottom, port_top, / port_near, port_far );

returns.doc: true if input is valid.

methods.name: TargetDistance
methods.signature: public double TargetDistance(bool useFrustumCenterFallback)
methods.summary: Gets the distance from the target point to the camera plane. Note that if the frustum is not symmetric, then this distance is shorter than the distance from the target to the camera location.

params.name: useFrustumCenterFallback
params.doc: If bUseFrustumCenterFallback is false and the target point is not valid, then ON_UNSET_VALUE is returned. If bUseFrustumCenterFallback is true and the frustum is valid and current target point is not valid or is behind the camera, then 0.5*(near + far) is returned.

returns.doc: Shortest signed distance from camera plane to target point. If the target point is on the visible side of the camera, a positive value is returned. ON_UNSET_VALUE is returned when the input of view is not valid.

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.signature: public string ToJSON(SerializationOptions options)
methods.summary: Create a JSON string representation of this object

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Runtime.CommonObject.ToJSON(Rhino.FileIO.SerializationOptions)"]

methods.name: TransformCamera
methods.signature: public bool TransformCamera(Transform xform)
methods.summary: Transforms the view camera location, direction, and up.

returns.doc: True if a valid camera was transformed, false if invalid camera, frustum, or transformation.

methods.name: UnlockCamera
methods.signature: public void UnlockCamera()
methods.summary: Unlocks the camera vectors and location.

methods.name: UnlockFrustumSymmetry
methods.signature: public void UnlockFrustumSymmetry()
methods.summary: Unlocks frustum horizontal and vertical symmetries.

methods.name: ZoomToScreenRect(Rectangle)
methods.signature: public bool ZoomToScreenRect(Rectangle windowRectangle)
methods.summary: Zooms to a screen zone. View changing from screen input points. Handy for using a mouse to manipulate a view. ZoomToScreenRect() may change camera and frustum settings.

params.name: windowRectangle
params.doc: The new window rectangle in screen space.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: ZoomToScreenRect(Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public bool ZoomToScreenRect(int left, int top, int right, int bottom)
methods.summary: Zooms to a screen zone. View changing from screen input points. Handy for using a mouse to manipulate a view. ZoomToScreenRect() may change camera and frustum settings.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

properties.name: Camera35mmLensLength
properties.summary: This property assumes the camera is horizontal and crop the film rather than the image when the aspect of the frustum is not 36/24. (35mm film is 36mm wide and 24mm high.) Setting preserves camera location, changes the frustum, but maintains the frustum's aspect.

properties.name: CameraAngle
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 1/2 smallest angle. See GetCameraAngles(Double, Double, Double) for more information.

properties.name: CameraDirection
properties.summary: Gets the direction that the camera faces.

properties.name: CameraLocation
properties.summary: Gets the camera location (position) point.

properties.name: CameraUp
properties.summary: Gets the camera up vector.

properties.name: CameraX
properties.summary: Gets the unit "to the right" vector.

properties.name: CameraY
properties.summary: Gets the unit "up" vector.

properties.name: CameraZ
properties.summary: Gets the unit vector in -CameraDirection.

properties.name: DefaultCameraDirection
properties.summary: Default z=up perspective camera direction

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: FrustumAspect
properties.summary: Setting FrustumAspect changes the larger of the frustum's width/height so that the resulting value of width/height matches the requested aspect. The camera angle is not changed. If you change the shape of the view port with a call SetScreenPort(), then you generally want to call SetFrustumAspect() with the value returned by GetScreenPortAspect().

properties.name: FrustumBottom
properties.summary: Gets the frustum bottom value. This is -top if the frustum has a horizontal symmetry axis. This number is usually negative.

properties.name: FrustumBottomPlane
properties.summary: Gets the frustum bottom plane that separates visible from off-screen.

properties.name: FrustumCenter
properties.summary: Gets the frustum center point.

properties.name: FrustumFar
properties.summary: Gets the frustum far-cutting value.

properties.name: FrustumFarPlane
properties.summary: Gets far clipping plane if camera and frustum are valid. The plane's frame is the same as the camera's frame. The origin is located at the intersection of the camera direction ray and the far clipping plane. The plane's normal points into the frustum towards the camera location.

properties.name: FrustumHeight
properties.summary: Gets the frustum height. This is FrustumTop - FrustumBottom.

properties.name: FrustumLeft
properties.summary: Gets the frustum left value. This is -right if the frustum has a vertical symmetry axis. This number is usually negative.

properties.name: FrustumLeftPlane
properties.summary: Gets the frustum left plane that separates visible from off-screen.

properties.name: FrustumMaximumDiameter
properties.summary: Gets the frustum maximum diameter, or the maximum between FrustumWidth and FrustumHeight.

properties.name: FrustumMinimumDiameter
properties.summary: Gets the frustum minimum diameter, or the minimum between FrustumWidth and FrustumHeight.

properties.name: FrustumNear
properties.summary: Gets the frustum near-cutting value.

properties.name: FrustumNearPlane
properties.summary: Gets near clipping plane if camera and frustum are valid. The plane's frame is the same as the camera's frame. The origin is located at the intersection of the camera direction ray and the near clipping plane. The plane's normal points out of the frustum towards the camera location.

properties.name: FrustumRight
properties.summary: Gets the frustum right value. This is -left if the frustum has a vertical symmetry axis. This number is usually positive.

properties.name: FrustumRightPlane
properties.summary: Gets the frustum right plane that separates visible from off-screen.

properties.name: FrustumTop
properties.summary: Gets the frustum top value. This is -bottom if the frustum has a horizontal symmetry axis. This number is usually positive.

properties.name: FrustumTopPlane
properties.summary: Gets the frustum top plane that separates visible from off-screen.

properties.name: FrustumWidth
properties.summary: Gets the frustum width. This is FrustumRight - FrustumLeft.

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: Id
properties.summary: Sets the viewport's id to the value used to uniquely identify this viewport. There is no approved way to change the viewport id once it is set in order to maintain consistency across multiple viewports and those routines that manage them.

properties.name: IsCameraDirectionLocked
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the direction that the camera faces is unmodifiable.

properties.name: IsCameraLocationLocked
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the camera location is unmodifiable.

properties.name: IsCameraUpLocked
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the camera up vector is unmodifiable.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsFrustumLeftRightSymmetric
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the camera frustum has a vertical symmetry axis.

properties.name: IsFrustumTopBottomSymmetric
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the camera frustum has a horizontal symmetry axis.

properties.name: IsParallelProjection
properties.summary: Get or set whether this projection is parallel.

properties.name: IsPerspectiveProjection
properties.summary: Get or set whether this projection is perspective.

properties.name: IsTwoPointPerspectiveProjection
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether this projection is a two-point perspective.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: IsValidCamera
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the camera is valid.

properties.name: IsValidFrustum
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the frustum is valid.

properties.name: PerspectiveMinNearDist
properties.summary: Expert user function to control the minimum value of near when perspective projections are being used.

properties.name: PerspectiveMinNearOverFar
properties.summary: Expert user function to control the minimum ratio of near/far when perspective projections are begin used.

properties.name: ScreenPort
properties.summary: Get or set the screen port. SetScreenPort(Rectangle) and

properties.name: ScreenPortAspect
properties.summary: Gets the screen aspect ratio. This is width / height.

properties.name: TargetPoint
properties.summary: The current value of the target point. This point does not play a role in the view projection calculations. It can be used as a fixed point when changing the camera so the visible regions of the before and after frustums both contain the region of interest. The default constructor sets this point on ON_3dPoint::UnsetPoint. You must explicitly call one SetTargetPoint() functions to set the target point.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.

properties.name: ViewScale
properties.summary: Applies scaling factors to parallel projection clipping coordinates by setting the m_clip_mod transformation. If you want to compress the view projection across the viewing plane, then set x = 0.5, y = 1.0, and z = 1.0.
